title: Run repair to maintain replica consistency
description: Schedule anti-entropy repair within gc_grace_seconds so deleted data does not resurrect across replicas.
body: Use to keep replicas consistent and prevent zombie data. Actions: run nodetool repair (or a managed reaper) on a schedule shorter than gc_grace_seconds so tombstones are reconciled across all replicas before they are garbage-collected, prefer incremental or subrange repair to bound load, and stagger repairs across the ring. Verify repair completes for every table within the gc_grace window. Validation signals include repair logs showing full coverage within the window and no resurrected deleted rows. Risk boundary: if a replica misses repair before gc_grace_seconds and a tombstone is collected, previously deleted data can reappear, so never let the repair interval exceed gc_grace_seconds and escalate a missed cycle.
